caption: The video appears to be an educational tutorial on cryogenic engineering, specifically focusing on the work done per unit mass of gas during compression. The slide displayed is titled "Tutorial - 4" and includes a formula: \(-\frac{W_c}{m} = T_i(s_1 - s_2) - (h_1 - h_2)\). This equation relates the work done during compression (\(W_c\)) to the temperature (\(T_i\)), entropy changes (\(s_1 - s_2\)), and enthalpy changes (\(h_1 - h_2\)) of the gas. The presentation is part of a series by Prof. M D Atrey from the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Bombay, as indicated at the bottom of the slide. The cursor briefly appears, suggesting that the presenter may be explaining or highlighting specific parts of the formula. The overall context suggests a detailed discussion on thermodynamic principles applied to cryogenic systems.
